message:
fix(no-db): re-read JSONL before flush to prevent clobbering concurrent writes

In --no-db mode, flush_no_db_if_dirty would export the in-memory snapshot
directly to the JSONL file. If another --no-db process had written to the
same JSONL between our initial read and the flush, those changes were
silently overwritten. Now we re-import the on-disk JSONL immediately before
exporting so that concurrent writes are merged via the existing last-write-wins
collision resolution.

Fixes #169

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
